verdicts.note: The evidence at docs.ramp.com/developer-api/v1/mcp explicitly describes 'The three Ramp MCP servers' including Ramp MCP, Developer MCP, and Ramp Data MCP.

The URL points to Ramp's own documentation page describing their MCP servers, not to a dedicated MCP server repository or implementation. This is documentation about their MCP servers, not a link to an actual MCP server codebase or package.

The evidence at docs.ramp.com/developer-api/v1/api/authorization confirms OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code, Refresh Token, and Client Credentials grants. Token endpoint at https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1/token uses Basic auth (base64 client_id:client_secret) and returns Bearer access token.

The quickstart guide states 'This guide assumes you are a Ramp customer with admin access to your dashboard' and shows admin must create app via Company → Developer menu. Pricing page shows Free tier exists but API access requires business account setup, supporting 'admin-approval' classification.
Evidence shows REST API with curl examples using standard HTTP methods (POST /developer/v1/token, GET /developer/v1/transactions).

The MCP page mentions 'transactions', quickstart shows transactions endpoint, and the authorization page references multiple scopes (users:read, users:write, transactions:read). Original record states 'transactions, cards, reimbursements, bills, users, etc.' which is consistent with a broad finance API.

Evidence supports 'buildable-with-caveats': requires Ramp business account with admin access (per quickstart guide), app registration for OAuth credentials, and sandbox is mentioned with 'Production write requests are disabled' caveat.

The quickstart explicitly requires 'admin access to your dashboard' and states users must be 'a Ramp customer'. Sandbox access requires a request ('Request sandbox access'), and production write operations are restricted. This supports the stated blocker.

Evidence confirms OAuth2-secured REST API (authorization page), MCP servers existence (MCP page lists three servers), and broad finance data access (transactions, cards, etc. implied by scopes and endpoints).

revised.name: Ramp
revised.one_liner: Ramp offers a broad OAuth2-secured REST Developer API for spend/finance data, plus separate MCP servers for documentation and account data access.

auth_methods.notes: OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code, Refresh Token, and Client Credentials grants. Token endpoint at https://api.ramp.com/developer/v1/token, authenticated via HTTP Basic (base64 client_id:client_secret), returns Bearer access token.

revised.self_serve_notes: Requires a Ramp business account and app registration (client ID/secret) to obtain OAuth credentials; production API access may require admin permissions or a support/provisioning request. Sandbox available for testing without full production approval.

api_surface.notes: Ramp Developer API (v1) covers business financial data: transactions, cards, reimbursements, bills, users, etc. OpenAPI spec published at /openapi/developer-api.json.

revised.main_blocker: Production API access requires a Ramp business account, OAuth app registration, and admin access; write operations are disabled in sandbox.

evidence.supports: OAuth2 flow details: client credentials, authorization code, refresh token grants; Basic auth for token endpoint; Bearer token usage.
Existence of Ramp MCP server(s) - three variants: Ramp MCP (account-specific), Developer MCP (unauthenticated docs), and Ramp Data MCP (public benchmarks).
Setup guide showing admin access required for app creation and OAuth credential provisioning.
Developer API access tied to business account plans, with Free tier available but requiring account setup.

flags: MCP servers confirmed to exist (three variants: Ramp MCP, Developer MCP, and Ramp Data MCP)
Requires business account with admin access for API credential provisioning
Sandbox environment available but write operations disabled, limiting testing depth
Free tier available but not truly self-serve without business account setup
